rulings.Case Title: JONATHAN S. STORPER VS. STEVEN DILLICK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Order Setting Aside Dismissal
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 8, 2012, Line 1, PLAINTIFF JONATHAN STORPER Notice Of Motion For Order Setting Aside Dismissal. Granted. The Court did not receive courtesy copies of the opposition papers as required by Local Rule 2.6B. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DOLLY TOTES VS. CITY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A PUBLIC ENTITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Deft To Form Interoggs, Set One
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday March 8, 2012, Line 1, PLAINTIFF DOLLY TOTES' Mtn To Compel Deft To Form Interoggs, Set One Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted. Defendant Barbara Jean Brooks to provide a verification to her original responses to the form interrogatories at issue by March 16, 2012. While Defendant's opposition contains responses with a verification, the verification is to "amended" responses, rather than the original responses. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: JUAN JORGE VS. SHIZUE ENTERPRISES INC., A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Entry Of Judgmnt
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 8, 2012, Line 5, PLAINTIFF JUAN JORGE'S Motion For Entry Of Judgment. Granted in part. Plaintiff is entitled to entry of judgment for the settlement amount, but not for any fees or costs since the agreement does not provide for an award of fees or costs and section 128.5 (mistakenly referred to in plaintiff's memorandum as 128.6) is inapplicable to this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the court will sign the proposed judgment submitted with the moving papers and convert one copy of the proposed judgment into an order and sign it, but neither the judgment nor the order will include any fees or costs. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: INFAC MANAGEMENT CORPORATION, VS. COMDISCO INVESTMENT GROUP, INC. et al

Granted in part and denied in part. Plaintiff's request for terminating sanctions and/or issue sanctions is denied. Defendant Charles Loucks is ordered to reappear for deposition and answer the questions set forth in Plaintiff's Separate Statement. Defendant Charles Loucks and his attorney, Terry Gross, must pay $4500 no later than April 6, 2012. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: BARRY G. INGRAM VS. 24HR FITNESS CENTERS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 8, 2012, Line 10, DEFENDANTS JIM ROWLEY, BOBBI QUICK, JUAN RAMIREZ, TYLER HANSEN, 24 HOUR FITNESS USA, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled as to the discrimination cause of action, sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the breach of contract cause of action, and sustained without leave to amend as to the fraud cause of action. The discrimination cause of action is adequately alleged. As to the breach of contract cause of action, Plaintiff is given leave to allege whether the contract was written and what terms were violated. The fraud claim fails to allege justifiable reliance and it does not appear that this failure can be cured by an amendment. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 8, 2012, Line 6, PLAINTIFF CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC., DBA GREAT APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER- Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required for Plaintiff to produce evidence showing Mary Dudum's ownership interest or role in the business entities at issue. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =302/HK

rulings.Case Title: CAFE TIRAMISU LLC, A LIMITED LIABILITY et al VS. WAH CHAI LIAN AKA ALBERT LIAN, AN INDIVIDUAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 8, 2012, Line 14, PLAINTIFFS CAFE TIRAMISU LLC, ANTO RESTAURANT GROUP LLC, THE NAPOLI GROUP LLC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled in its entirety. The challenged affirmative defenses are either adequately pled and/or the demurrer as to them serves no useful purpose. Any legitimate need for further information that plaintiffs have can be satisfied by propounding discovery. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ANDREW HAWKINS-COHEN VS. KHONG TU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Nonstatutory Partial Judgment On The Pleadings As To First Count Of The X-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 8, 2012, Line 16, CROSS COMPLAINANTS KHONG TU, KHY LOR'S Motion For Nonstatutory Partial Judgment On The Pleadings As To First Count Of The Cross-Complaint. Granted with ten days leave to file an amended answer. Plaintiff is granted leave, if he can do so in good faith, to allege one or more affirmative defenses that subsequent to the foreclosure the lease he had prior to the foreclosure was reinstated and/or that the such lease called for payment of rent that was not substantially less than market rent. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: ANDREW HAWKINS-COHEN VS. KHONG TU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Reconsideration; Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 8, 2012, Line 16, PLAINTIFF ANDREW HAWKINS-COHE'S Motion For Reconsideration; Request For Judicial Notice. Denied. After yet another full review of the parties' positions on defendant's anti-SLAPP motion, the court remains of the view that the motion was correctly granted. Defendant satisfied his burden that non-incidental allegations in each of plaintiff's claims arise out of activitoes protected by the anti-SLAPP statute and plaintiff has not shown that any of his claims have merit. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: GLORIA ARGUETA VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUNICIPAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 8, 2012, Line 18, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Granted. The filing of the amended complaint did not moot the motion. (Sylmar AirConditioning v. Pueblo contracting Services, Inc. (2004) 122 Cal.App.4th 1049). The complaint arises from petitioning activity and plaintiff fails to establish a probability of prevailing on her claims. Defendant to recover $4,280 in fees. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
